title: Residential Substance Use Disorder Treatment Facilities * Women - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Residential Substance Use Disorder Treatment Facilities * Women 6 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adolescents Deafness Fathers Men Offender/Ex-Offender Issues Women Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CITYTEAM RESTORATIVE RESIDENTIAL WOMEN'S PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides long-term residential substance use disorder treatment including individual case management, recovery-based classes, community involvement, and other services. Program stays typically last about 6 months but participants will have the option of an additional 24 month program. Eligibility: Women age 18 and older who self-identify as having a substance use disorder. This program is not suitable for clients who need medically assisted or dual diagnosis treatments or have a sex offender conviction. Hours: Intake: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm (503) 231-9334 4180 Visit Website More Details CODA BEAVERTON RECOVERY CENTER WOMEN'S RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers gender-specific services for women, including those who are pregnant and parenting, and women involved with child welfare or criminal justice systems. Treatment supports women as they work on parenting in recovery and building and supporting safe and stable families and homes. Access to methadone services available to program participants. 17911 NW Evergreen Place Beaverton, OR 97006 Eligibility: Women age 18 and older Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 239-8400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CODA GRESHAM RECOVERY CENTER WOMEN’S AND MEN’S RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT WOMEN'S RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers gender-specific services for women, including those who are pregnant and parenting, and women involved with child welfare or criminal justice systems. Treatment supports women as they work on parenting in recovery and building and supporting safe and stable families and homes. Access to methadone services available to program participants. 1427 SE 182nd Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Women age 18 and older; may include patients with cases in child welfare or criminal justice systems. Children may stay with their mothers on site. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 761-6005 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND RESCUE MISSION PORTLAND RESCUE MISSION SHEPHERD'S DOOR SHEPHERD'S DOOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Portland Rescue Mission offers healing and life growth through Christian-based, trauma-informed care. At Shepherd's Door, up to 42 women and mothers with their children overcome substance use disorders, and work towards self-sufficiency in a retreat-like setting. Shepherd's Door residential programs begin with Discovery, a 5-week assessment, stabilization, and relapse prevention program. Discovery graduates are eligible to enter New Life, a one-year holistic recovery program. Onsite child care and programming for children is provided along with parenting support. Transition programs are available upon completion of New Life. 13207 NE Halsey Street Portland, OR 97230 Eligibility: Women age 18 and older and women with children age 10 and younger Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 746-9743 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNION GOSPEL MISSION ANGELA'S HOUSE LIFECHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides faith-based transitional housing. Guests will have access to their own room, meals, an education center, a children's activity center, a commercial kitchen, a counseling center, and a chapel. 18555 NW Rock Creek Boulevard Portland, OR 97229 Eligibility: Men and women (including women with children) Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 802-2271 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WILLAMETTE FAMILY INC WILLAMETTE FAMILY SUNRISE HOUSE & FAMILIES IN RECOVERY WOMEN'S RESIDENTIAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides residential substance abuse treatment. Eligibility: Females age 18 and and female individuals with children ages 0-5 Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (541) 762-4300 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CODA WOMEN'S RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT CODA WOMEN'S RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT PORTLAND RESCUE MISSION SHEPHERD'S DOOR UNION GOSPEL MISSION LIFECHANGE
